new-fantasy: New Fantasy is a mobile AR, real-time multiplayer PvE game that mixes first-person combat with real-world movement, asking players to aim, dodge, squat, and run their way through fights against fantastical creatures.</br></br>I joined mid-project on a small team alongside a lead programmer, a designer, a 3D artist and a 2D artist. My main work was in Unity, developing the core meta-game UI systems, lobby flows and incorporating IAPs, I also helped with backend integration which was implemented with Node.js and MongoDB, and AR gameplay features with AR Foundation and Apple AR Cloud Anchors. I also helped prototype a geolocation/map layer with POIs, trails, and a quest system. The shipped version was released on iOS.
devils-purge: Devil’s Purge is a single-player AR roguelike shooter built for mobile, where players physically move through their environment to dodge attacks, reposition, and target enemy weak spots in combat.</br>Across procedurally generated enemy waves, escalating encounters, and three boss fights, players collect blessings and abilities from NPCs to shape each run. The game also featured a location-based map layer with real-world POIs, where players could interact with NPCs to receive random upgrades.</br></br>As the sole programmer on the project, I was responsible for the full technical implementation across gameplay, UI, progression, inventory, shop, and narrative systems. I used Unity Visual Scripting and state machines with custom nodes to support iteration by design, as well as a dynamic dialogue system that reacted to player data and in-game progression. The team moved quickly from prototype to a shipped App Store release in about 9 months time.
cyberload: This prototype, developed within a two-month window, is a first-person RPG dungeon crawler set in a surreal cyber-fantasy world, with self-generated dungeons, permadeath, and a rotating cast of seekers with different stats and builds.</br>Players hold to move forward through on-rails dungeon paths, branch at crossroads with a wheel-based selection system, fight randomly encountered monsters, and pick up spells, abilities, and shrine upgrades as they push toward the end of each run.</br></br>I worked as the sole programmer on a small team with a designer, 3D artist, and 2D artist, and built the UI, combat flow, enemy systems, dungeon traversal, and other gameplay logic.
